problems.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
problems.prompt:
Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array.

Note that it is the kth largest element in the sorted order, not the kth distinct element. Solve it with a heap (or quickselect) — sorting the whole array is accepted for interview practice but prefer O(n log k).

problems.title: Last Stone Weight
problems.prompt:
You are given an array of integers stones where stones[i] is the weight of the ith stone.

We continually smash the heaviest two stones. Suppose the heaviest two are x and y with x <= y. If x == y both are destroyed; otherwise stone of weight y - x is put back. Return the smallest possible weight of the left stone. If there are no stones left, return 0.

problems.title: K Closest Points to Origin
problems.prompt:
Given an array of points where points[i] = [xi, yi] represents a point on the X-Y plane and an integer k, return the k closest points to the origin (0, 0).

The distance is Euclidean: sqrt(xi^2 + yi^2). You may return the answer in any order. The answer is guaranteed to be unique (except for order).

problems.title: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.prompt: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
constraints: k is in the range [1, number of unique elements]
It is guaranteed the answer is unique

problems.title: Merge K Sorted Lists (Lite)
problems.prompt:
You are given an array of k linked-list heads, each sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked lists into one sorted linked list and return its head.

Use a min-heap of list heads. Constraints are lite: total nodes <= 10^4.

problems.title: Reorganize String
problems.prompt: Given a string s, rearrange the characters of s so that any two adjacent characters are not the same. Return any possible rearrangement, or return "" if not possible.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters

solution.python:
class Solution:
    def reorganizeString(self, s: str) -> str:
        from collections import Counter
        import heapq
        heap = [(-c, ch) for ch, c in Counter(s).items()]
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        out = []
        prev = None
        while heap:
            cnt, ch = heapq.heappop(heap)
            out.append(ch)
            if prev:
                heapq.heappush(heap, prev)
            prev = (cnt + 1, ch) if cnt + 1 < 0 else None
        return ''.join(out) if len(out) == len(s) else ''

problems.title: Find Median from Data Stream
problems.prompt:
The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. If the size is even, the median is the mean of the two middle values.

Implement MedianFinder:
- MedianFinder(): Initialize
- void addNum(int num): Add integer from the data stream
- double findMedian(): Return the median of all elements so far

Use two heaps (max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half).
constraints: There will be at least one element before findMedian
At most 5 * 10^4 calls to addNum and findMedian

problems.title: Task Scheduler (Lite)
problems.prompt:
You are given an array of CPU tasks, each represented by a character from A to Z, and a non-negative integer n representing the cooldown between two same tasks. Each unit of time the CPU executes one task or is idle.

Return the least number of units of times that the CPU will take to finish all the given tasks.

Lite constraints: tasks.length <= 1000.
constraints: tasks[i] is an uppercase English letter
